extDesc.message: Shows whether the current page is direct, proxied or blocked using the compatible control API in Clash, Mihomo and sing-box

updateHighlights.message: Improved onboarding languages with clearer router, Zashboard, and compatible-core setup guidance.

errHint.message: Cannot reach the compatible controller: $ERR$. Check the address, secret, and the control API settings in Clash, Mihomo, or sing-box.

noRecord.message: No connection record for $HOST$. Reload the page, then reopen this popup.

setupHint.message: First run: enter a Clash-compatible controller address and secret. Clash, Mihomo, sing-box, and other cores exposing the same API are supported.

bgNotConfigured.message: Clash Indicator: not configured yet — click the icon to set up

wIntro.message: Complete three quick steps, then the icon shows whether the current page is direct, proxied, blocked, or temporarily unknown.

wStep1Desc.message: Click the puzzle icon 🧩 in the toolbar, find "Clash Indicator", and click the pin next to it. Without pinning, you would have to dig through the menu every time.

wStep2Desc.message: Enter the external controller address and secret from Clash, Mihomo, sing-box, or another compatible core. Test the connection, then save.

controllerHelp.message: If the proxy runs on your router, a common address is http://router-lan-ip:9090, such as http://192.168.1.1:9090. For a local app, http://127.0.0.1:9090 is common. Always use the address and port from your actual configuration.

dashboardHelp.message: If Zashboard or another dashboard opens at http://router-ip:9090/ui/, enter the base address without /ui/ here: http://router-ip:9090.

wStep3Desc.message: Open any page and watch the toolbar icon. Click it to inspect rules, resource routes, and request duration:

helpCoresTitle.message: Enable the control API in each core

helpCoresIntro.message: The extension connects directly to a Clash-compatible API and does not depend on Zashboard. Restart or reload your proxy core after changing its configuration.

clashDesc.message: Add or confirm these fields in your Clash configuration. Use 127.0.0.1 for a local core; adjust the listen address when the core runs on a router.

mihomoDesc.message: Mihomo uses the same YAML fields. Router and OpenWrt packages often enable the controller already, so try deriving it from the existing dashboard URL first.

lanSecurity.message: Listening on 0.0.0.0 allows LAN access. Set a strong secret, limit access to your trusted LAN, and never expose this port to the public internet.

singboxDesc.message: Enable the compatible control API under experimental.clash_api in your sing-box configuration. The extension only uses this compatible API; running Clash itself is not required.

findAddressIntro.message: You need the base URL containing the protocol, host, and port, such as http://192.168.1.1:9090. Port 9090 is common, but your configuration is authoritative.

findFromDashboard.message: Derive it from a dashboard: if Zashboard opens at http://192.168.1.1:9090/ui/, remove /ui/ and enter http://192.168.1.1:9090.

findOnRouter.message: Core on a router: note the router's LAN IP, then check the proxy package's external controller, API, or dashboard settings for the port.

findOnLocal.message: Core on this computer: try http://127.0.0.1:9090 first. If it fails, search the app settings or config for external-controller / external_controller.

findInConfig.message: Confirm the secret in the same configuration area. Enter its raw value, not a dashboard password, and do not keep /ui/ at the end of the address.

faqUnreachableTitle.message: Cannot connect or Failed to fetch

faqUnreachableDesc.message: Check that the core is running and the protocol and port are correct. For access from another device, the controller cannot listen only on 127.0.0.1, and the firewall must allow the port from your trusted LAN.

faqUnauthorizedDesc.message: The controller is reachable, but the secret does not match. Check the configured secret again, including spaces and letter case.

faqHttpsTitle.message: HTTPS or a self-signed certificate fails

faqHttpsDesc.message: Open the controller address directly in the browser and trust its certificate before testing again. Also confirm whether the address uses http or https.

dashboardSettingHelp.message: Works with any core or standalone Zashboard URL. When empty, the controller URL + /ui/ is used.

bgNoRecord.message: $HOST$: no connection record, reload the page and retry
